data.content_ref: inline:The account claims the collapse was caused by a systemic failure of the palatial trade and diplomacy network, yet it excludes Egypt from the scope of collapse while simultaneously relying on Egypt's survival as evidence for the mechanism. This creates an internal contradiction: if the network was truly tightly coupled and a shock to one node would cascade through the entire system, Egypt should have been equally susceptible. The account does not explain why Egypt's node in this network was decoupled enough to survive the cascading failure that supposedly brought down all other nodes, nor does it specify what structural feature of Egypt's palatial economy made it immune to the very systemic risk it posits as the primary cause.
